response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, seasoned engineer with 15+ years of experience, current big-tech tenure (Staff at Google, previously Meta), high-scale distributed systems work, and relevant AI-tooling exposure (MCP, agentic workflows). However, the role's core domain — experimentation platforms, feature flighting, telemetry/metrics infrastructure, and statistical analysis — is entirely absent from the resume, and there is little evidence of principal-level, multi-team architectural leadership. Career history also shows inconsistencies (e.g., a 'Founder, Amazon' entry that appears to be a mislabeled personal project) that warrant clarification.
strengths: 15+ years of broad engineering experience including Google (Staff) and Meta, exceeding the 8+ year requirement
Hands-on high-scale distributed systems work (low-latency services handling millions of requests/sec)
Recent AI-agent infrastructure and developer-tooling experience (MCP code generators, agentic workflows) matching a preferred qualification
concerns: No demonstrated experimentation, A/B testing, feature-flagging, or growth-measurement platform experience — the role's central domain
No evidence of principal-level scope: leading multi-workstream, cross-team initiatives or platform architecture used by many teams
Resume inconsistencies (title mismatch, 'Founder, Amazon' for an open-source side project, overlapping dates) reduce signal reliability

key_factors.factor: Strong tenure and scale at Google/Meta with distributed systems
